justification: B outperforms A on every dimension. On skill_accuracy, both standardize abbreviations correctly, but A includes 'Artificial Intelligence' removed in favor of the more specific 'Machine Learning' — however A also adds 'ETL' and 'REST APIs' and 'SQLAlchemy' and 'Socket.io' as standalone skills, slightly bloating beyond what's expected; B is cleaner and equally accurate. On conservatism, A extracts 24 skills which exceeds the 8-12 target range for this candidate, while B's 22 is still slightly high but closer and better justified. On project_classification, B correctly classifies all three projects (NLP/GenAI for BERT sentiment tool, Full-Stack Web App for chat app, Data Engineering/ETL for internship work), while A extracts no project classifications at all (scored neutral 3). On impact_quality, B captures concrete metrics (80,000 tweets, AWS EC2 deployment, GCP Cloud Run deployment) with specific dimensions, while A provides no impact highlights. B is the clear winner across all meaningful dimensions.
